questions.q: In which city is Bibi Ka Maqbara located?
questions.explanation: Bibi Ka Maqbara stands in Aurangabad (now Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar) in Maharashtra.

questions.q: Bibi Ka Maqbara is popularly known by which nickname?
questions.explanation: Because it closely resembles the Taj Mahal, it is widely called the 'Taj of the Deccan'.

questions.q: Which famous monument was Bibi Ka Maqbara modelled on?
questions.explanation: The mausoleum was deliberately designed to imitate the Taj Mahal at Agra, though on a smaller scale.

questions.q: Bibi Ka Maqbara is a tomb (mausoleum) associated with which Mughal emperor?
questions.explanation: It was built for the wife of the Mughal emperor Aurangzeb.

questions.q: In which century was Bibi Ka Maqbara built?
questions.explanation: The mausoleum was constructed in the 17th century during Aurangzeb's reign.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state is Bibi Ka Maqbara situated?
questions.explanation: Aurangabad, the home of Bibi Ka Maqbara, is in the state of Maharashtra.

questions.q: What type of structure is Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: Bibi Ka Maqbara is a mausoleum built over a grave, much like the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Compared with the Taj Mahal, Bibi Ka Maqbara is generally described as:
questions.explanation: It was built as a smaller-scale imitation of the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What is the central feature crowning the main tomb structure of Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: Like the Taj Mahal, the building is topped by a large bulbous central dome.

questions.q: How many minarets surround the main tomb of Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: Four tall minarets stand at the corners of the platform, echoing the Taj Mahal's layout.

questions.q: For whom was Bibi Ka Maqbara built?
questions.explanation: It was raised as the tomb of Dilras Banu Begum, Aurangzeb's first and principal wife.

questions.q: What was the name of the woman buried in Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: Dilras Banu Begum, who was given the posthumous title Rabia-ud-Daurani, is interred there.

questions.q: By what posthumous title was Aurangzeb's wife, buried here, also known?
questions.explanation: Dilras Banu Begum was honoured posthumously as Rabia-ud-Daurani.

questions.q: Bibi Ka Maqbara was commissioned by whom?
questions.explanation: It is generally credited to Azam Shah, Aurangzeb's son, in memory of his mother, during Aurangzeb's reign.

questions.q: Which material is used to face the central upper portion and dome of the main tomb?
questions.explanation: Marble was used mainly for the central dome and upper part, with cheaper materials lower down.

questions.q: Why is Bibi Ka Maqbara considered less grand than the Taj Mahal in its materials?
options: Marble was used sparingly, with plaster used over much of it
questions.explanation: Limited funds meant marble was reserved for the dome and key areas, with lime plaster (stucco) covering much of the rest.

questions.q: Which architect is traditionally credited with designing Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: The architect is traditionally named as Ata-ullah, said to be the son of the Taj Mahal's architect Ustad Ahmad Lahori.

questions.q: The layout of Bibi Ka Maqbara is set within what kind of garden plan?
questions.explanation: The tomb sits within a formal char bagh, the four-quartered Mughal garden also used at the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: Bibi Ka Maqbara was completed in roughly which period?
questions.explanation: Construction was carried out in the latter half of the 17th century, completed around the 1660s-1680s.

questions.q: What lies directly beneath the central chamber, holding the actual grave?
options: A lower burial chamber reached by steps
questions.explanation: The actual grave lies in a lower chamber below floor level, with an octagonal screen surrounding it, similar to the Taj.

questions.q: Which body now maintains Bibi Ka Maqbara as a protected monument?
questions.explanation: Bibi Ka Maqbara is a protected monument under the Archaeological Survey of India (ASI).

questions.q: Aurangabad, where Bibi Ka Maqbara stands, was historically important as a base for which campaigns of Aurangzeb?
questions.explanation: Aurangabad served as a key Mughal centre during Aurangzeb's long Deccan campaigns in southern India.

questions.q: Dilras Banu Begum, buried in Bibi Ka Maqbara, belonged to which royal lineage?
questions.explanation: Dilras Banu Begum was a Safavid princess, daughter of Mirza Badi-uz-Zaman Safavi, a Persian noble.

questions.q: Azam Shah, who built the tomb for his mother Dilras Banu Begum, did what shortly after Aurangzeb's death in 1707?
options: Renounced all titles and became a hermit
Briefly proclaimed himself Mughal emperor before being defeated
Fled to Persia and never returned
Was crowned a long-reigning emperor for thirty years
questions.explanation: Muhammad Azam Shah, son of Aurangzeb and Dilras Banu Begum, proclaimed himself emperor in 1707 but was defeated and killed at the Battle of Jajau the same year.

questions.q: Compared with the Taj Mahal's pietra dura, Bibi Ka Maqbara relies more heavily on which decorative technique?
questions.explanation: Owing to a limited budget, much of the decoration is fine stucco plasterwork rather than costly inlaid marble.

questions.q: Dilras Banu Begum, for whom the tomb was built, died as a result of what?
options: A long illness in old age
questions.explanation: She died in 1657 from complications after the birth of her son Muhammad Akbar.

questions.q: The marble used for the dome of Bibi Ka Maqbara was reportedly brought from quarries near which place?
questions.explanation: Marble for the structure is traditionally said to have come from the Makrana quarries of Rajasthan, the same source famed for the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What is the approximate height of the main mausoleum building of Bibi Ka Maqbara?
questions.explanation: The mausoleum rises roughly 21 metres (about 70 feet), markedly shorter than the Taj Mahal.

questions.q: What is the UNESCO World Heritage status of Bibi Ka Maqbara itself?
options: It is a UNESCO World Heritage Site
It is not a UNESCO World Heritage Site
It is a UNESCO natural site
questions.explanation: Bibi Ka Maqbara is an ASI-protected monument but is not inscribed as a UNESCO World Heritage Site, unlike the nearby Ajanta and Ellora caves.

questions.q: An inscription at Bibi Ka Maqbara records the name of which superintendent/engineer involved in its construction?
questions.explanation: An inscription names the architect Ata-ullah and the engineer Hanspat Rai as those responsible for the work.
